metadata.author: Nahum
metadata.subject: The Judgment of Jehovah upon Nineveh, the Capital of the Cruel Assyrian Empire.

1: The burden of Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
Jehovah is a jealous and avenging God; Jehovah is avenging and full of wrath; Jehovah takes vengeance on His adversaries and keeps wrath for His enemies.
Jehovah is slow to anger and great in power, and He will by no means clear the guilty. Jehovah has His way in the whirlwind and in the storm, and the clouds are the dust of His feet.
Jehovah is good, a stronghold in the day of distress; and He knows those who take refuge in Him.
2: The scatterer has come up against you. Guard the fortress! Watch the way! Strengthen your loins! Fortify your power greatly!
Behold, I am against you, declares Jehovah of hosts; and I will burn up her chariots in smoke, and the sword will devour your young lions.
3: Woe to the city of blood! It is all full of lies and robbery; the prey does not depart.
There is no healing for your wound; your injury is fatal. All who hear the report about you clap their hands over you; for upon whom has your wickedness not passed continually?

7.text: While God is a consuming fire to His enemies, He remains a place of absolute safety and recognition for those who trust in Him.

1.text: Nineveh was notorious for its extreme cruelty and military aggression. This judgment marks the end of an empire built on violence.

19.text: The fall of Nineveh was met with universal relief by the surrounding nations because of the ceaseless oppression they had suffered at Assyria's hand.
